title: Long-Distance Elder Care And Verified Local Response In Viet Nam
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying older people whose families cannot respond quickly and testing whether a consented local care network closes routine and urgent needs.
why_it_matters: Remote monitoring can notify a distant family without delivering care. Viet Nam needs evidence on the denominator, responder network, consent, reliability, and cost before any technology-mediated service is treated as protective.

success_metrics.target: Every estimate separates living arrangement, family travel time, functional need, and local responder availability.
success_metrics.validation_method: A replicator reconstructs the denominator from documented source fields and exclusion rules.

success_metrics.target: Every field-test escalation is classified as acknowledged, reached, resolved, transferred, or unresolved.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer traces every escalation from timestamped request to consented closure evidence.

success_metrics.target: No monitoring or family disclosure occurs without recorded, revocable consent or a documented lawful exception.
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer audits consent, opt-out, proxy authority, data minimization, and complaint handling.

success_metrics.target: No monitoring, safety, or health-benefit claim advances without the evidence level required for that claim.
success_metrics.validation_method: Domain reviewer checks that operational results are not presented as clinical outcome evidence.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, red-team and field-reality review, and independent replication before any field-facing recommendation.
